briefdescription.para: < Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros

memberdef.name: XHDCP1X_PORT_UINT_TO_BUF
memberdef.initializer: if ((numbits) > 0) { \ int byte; \ for (byte = 0; byte <= (int)(((numbits) - 1) >> 3); byte++) { \ buf[byte] = (uint8_t) (Value & 0xFFu); \ Value >>= 8; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro converts from an unsigned integer to a little endian formatted buffer.

parameterdescription.para: the number of bits within the unsigned integer to use

simplesect.para: The value of the "Value" parameter is destroyed by a call to this macro

memberdef.name: XHDCP1X_PORT_BUF_TO_UINT
memberdef.initializer: if ((numbits) > 0) { \ int byte; \ Value = 0; \ for (byte = (((numbits) - 1) >> 3); byte >= 0; byte--) { \ Value <<= 8; \ Value |= buf[byte]; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This macro converts from a little endian formatted buffer to an unsigned integer value.

parameterdescription.para: the number of bits within the buffer to use

briefdescription.para: This macro sets a bit within a little endian formatted buffer.
This macro clears a bit within a little endian formatted buffer.
This macro tests a bit within a little endian formatted buffer.

parameterdescription.para: the buffer containing the bit to test

briefdescription.para: An (re)auth request.
This typedef defines the different types of handlers that can be registered to service interrupt requests from the HDCP port instance.
The typedef defines the HDCP port adaptor table.

detaileddescription.para: This contains a series of functions that map the external interface of the HDCP port device to the underlying physical interface that it is running over

briefdescription.para: This function determines the adaptor for a specified port device.

parameterdescription.para: is the device whose adaptor is to be determined.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the adaptor table. NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDCP port instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function enables a port device.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if the port lacks an Enable function.

briefdescription.para: This function disables a port device.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if the port lacks a Disable function.

briefdescription.para: This function queries a port device to determine if hdcp is supported.
This function queries a port device to determine if it is connected to a repeater.
This function set the REPEATER information in the connected device.

parameterdescription.para: is the configuration of the device as repeater.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if the port fails to set the Repeater value.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the repeater information from the connected device.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if the port lacks a GetRepeaterInfo function.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XHdcp1x *InstancePtr, u8 Offset, void *Buf, u32 BufSize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp1x_PortRead

briefdescription.para: This function reads a register from a HDCP port device.

parameterdescription.para: is the device to read from.
is the offset to start reading from.
is the buffer to copy the data read.
is the size of the buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XHdcp1x *InstancePtr, u8 Offset, const void *Buf, u32 BufSize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp1x_PortWrite

briefdescription.para: This function writes a register within a HDCP port device.

parameterdescription.para: is the device to write to.
is the offset to start writing at.
is the buffer containing the data to write.
is the size of the buffer.

briefdescription.para: This handles an interrupt generated by a HDCP port device.

parameterdescription.para: is the device to write to.
is the interrupt cause bit map.

briefdescription.para: This function enables encryption/decyption for all timeslots.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if the port lacks an Enable function.
